ddls:
CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., USA, United Kingdom) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the location belongs to (e.g., North America, Europe) */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent the location belongs to (e.g., North America, Asia) */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* Capital city of the location */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the location */,
  "area_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the location in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator */,
  "indicator_name" TEXT /* Name of the economic indicator (e.g., Top 100, % of World Population) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the economic indicator */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the economic indicator */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the economic indicator data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_data_source FOREIGN KEY ("data_source") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_indicators" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the economic indicator for the location */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., actual, estimated, forecasted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id", "indicator_id", "year")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location the company is in */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry the company operates in */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector the company operates in */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue of the company */,
  "employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_industry FOREIGN KEY ("industry") REFERENCES industries ("industry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "universities" (
  "university_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each university */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location the university is in */,
  "university_name" TEXT /* Name of the university */,
  "ranking" INTEGER /* Ranking of the university */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of university (e.g., public, private) */,
  "students" INTEGER /* Number of students in the university */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("university_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_universities_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry (e.g., Technology, Finance) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector the industry belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_department FOREIGN KEY ("department") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location being accessed */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the platform was accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the platform was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES economic_indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, analyst, admin) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department (e.g., Research, Analytics) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)
